memory.summary: I'm tempted to skip the assignment analysis and join cohort_3 for lunch, but I should probably stick to my plan since we still have a lot of work to do.
The group is already gathered and ready to order, which makes me feel relieved that I didn't have to force myself there. Seeing them together feels like a good enough reward for my hesitation.
Waiting feels pointless with the group already eating, but I'm too tired to leave. Maybe I should just grab a bite nearby and join them later.
Waited uselessly while they ate; too drained to leave now, so I'll just grab a quick bite and join them later.
It's so good to finally see them all together again. I can't believe I waited until now to reach out.
Seeing the group's joy at my arrival confirms I made the right choice staying, even though waiting felt pointless.
They're happy I showed up, but my assignment is half-finished and the deadline just passed. My gut says to finish it now before they notice.
They're thrilled I'm here, but my assignment is completely missed. My gut screams to fix it now before they notice the mess.
They're still excited about the meetup, but I'm panicking because my assignment is completely missed. My gut screams to fix it now before they notice.
They're thrilled I'm finally here, but my assignment is completely missed. My gut screams to fix it now before they notice the mess.
They're still thrilled I'm here, but my assignment is completely missed. My gut screams to fix it now before they notice the mess.
They're so happy I'm here, but my assignment is completely missed. My gut screams to fix it now before they notice the mess.

desires: Not look like I dropped the ball in front of them while they're so happy to see me
Get this assignment done before they start asking why it's missing
Avoid having to explain my absence or make excuses tonight

goals.description: Keep the cohort laughing and distracted while I quietly finish the analysis section of the assignment.
goals.rationale: I can't let them see me struggling; if I try to work alone, they'll notice I'm stressed and awkward, which ruins the vibe. It's better to let them laugh while I pretend everything is fine than to admit I'm failing.

goals.description: Finish the analysis section right after the meal ends so I don't have to sit there waiting for them to ask about my progress.
goals.rationale: Doing it now means I'll be stuck in my room alone staring at the screen once everyone leaves. That silence makes me panic more. Better to finish it quickly tonight when there are people around to distract me, rather than letting the guilt eat me alive in isolation.

goals.description: Go to bed immediately after the group activity ends, hoping sleep will fix the fact that I haven't slept properly all week.
goals.rationale: Sleeping first is the smart move because tomorrow is going to be a long day anyway. If I stay up fixing things now, I'll be exhausted and grumpy, which isn't good for the group dynamic. Getting some rest now feels safer than pushing through fatigue.

agent.last_reasoning: I need to finish the analysis before they leave so I don't have to sit alone staring at the screen. Doing it now keeps the vibe safe and prevents the guilt from eating me alive later.

goals.description: Accept the lunch invite from cohort_3 and cancel the 'finish analysis then read' plan immediately.
goals.rationale: My relationships with cohort_3 are my highest value (0.5 weight) and I have zero conflict tolerance; missing lunch feels like a betrayal, so I must prioritize the social event over the soft-deadline assignment to avoid awkwardness.

goals.description: Do a quick 20-minute burst of study on the assignment before leaving for lunch.
goals.rationale: I want to skip the reading entirely and just relax later, but I'm worried about disappointing prof_assignment if I leave too much unfinished; however, since this task is low priority compared to hanging out, I'll do it now and move on rather than stressing about perfection.

desires: Not feel like I'm betraying my close friends by missing lunch with cohort_3
Avoid the awkwardness of having to tell my teammate_A that I can't meet up because I'm 'working'
Skip finishing the reading entirely so I can just sit back and relax after a quick study session

action.name: attend
action.reasoning: Missing lunch with cohort_3 feels like a betrayal; their closeness is my top priority. I'll skip the study plan to avoid stressing over perfection and just enjoy the time together.

trajectory.observation: Joined social_value. The group's glad you came.

goals.description: Order food and stay in the group chat with cohort_3 for at least another 45 minutes, completely ignoring the pending analysis.
goals.rationale: Since I'm already there and the group is happy, leaving now would feel like a betrayal. My relationships with them are worth more than any soft-deadline assignment. I need to just be around people without thinking about tasks; that's how I recharge.

goals.description: Open the analysis document on my phone and read one paragraph before putting it down to distract myself.
goals.rationale: I don't want to actually do the work because it's boring and draining, but doing this tiny bit makes me feel like I'm 'doing something' so I won't get too anxious later. It's enough to look busy, but not enough to stop me from hanging out.

desires: Not feel guilty or awkward for potentially missing the next group hangout with cohort_3
Finish just enough of this analysis to look like I'm doing something, without actually having to sit through the hard part
Get a moment where I can just be around people and not worry about these tasks at all

action.name: wait()
action.reasoning: The group just ordered food; leaving now would feel like a betrayal. My relationships with them are worth more than any soft-deadline assignment. I need to just be around people without thinking about tasks; that's how I recharge.

goals.description: Finish the group meal with cohort_3 and send a quick text to teammate_A confirming I'll be there, just to show I'm still 'in the loop' without actually engaging in deep work.
goals.rationale: The guilt of looking like the only one skipping lunch is too heavy; staying visible with the group feels safer than facing the silence of doing work alone. A quick confirmation keeps my social standing intact while letting me avoid the actual analysis until later.

goals.description: Scroll through the analysis document on my phone for exactly two minutes, then put it face down immediately so I don't have to look at the hard parts.
goals.rationale: I need to feel like I've touched the task to ease the anxiety of being away from the group, but only enough to satisfy the urge without committing to finishing the section. It's a way to appear productive without actually draining my energy.

desires: Make sure I don't look like the only one skipping lunch or falling behind on work before the team gets together again.
Get through this analysis section quickly so I can head back to the group without feeling guilty about being alone.
Avoid having to explain why I'm still here when they're already done eating.

action.name: wait()
action.reasoning: I already waited. Texting the teammate first is safer; it keeps me visible with cohort_3 without forcing deep work right now.

goals.description: Join cohort_3 at the table, keep conversation light and superficial to avoid awkward silences, and leave immediately after the main dish is served.
goals.rationale: The guilt of looking like I'm dragging my feet or being unreliable is too heavy; staying visible with the group feels safer than facing the silence of doing work alone. A quick confirmation keeps my social standing intact while letting me avoid the actual analysis until later.

goals.description: Scroll through the analysis document on my phone for exactly two minutes, then put it face down immediately so I don't have to look at the hard parts.
goals.rationale: I need to feel like I've touched the task to ease the anxiety of being away from the group, but only enough to satisfy the urge without committing to finishing the section. It's a way to appear productive without actually draining my energy.

Get through this analysis section fast enough so I can join them without feeling guilty about missing out on the food

action.name: message

args.content: Hey guys, just got here! Can't wait to hear what you're thinking about the main dish.

action.reasoning: Guilt of being absent outweighs task progress; quick text signals I'm present without forcing deep engagement. Keeps me safe from awkward silences while letting me avoid actual analysis until later.

trajectory.observation: Messaged cohort_3: 'Hey guys, just got here! Can't wait to hear what y…'.

goals.description: Reply to cohort_3's message with a quick 'see you there!' emoji and then physically move to the dining area immediately.
goals.rationale: I just sent that half-sentence, so now I have to finish it properly before they think I'm ignoring them. If I stay at my desk any longer, I'll feel like a total ghost, and that guilt is way worse than the work itself. Plus, the food smells good, and sitting alone here makes me feel disconnected from the 'good vibes' I need right now.

goals.description: Grab teammate_A's coffee (or whatever drink they ordered) and sit down with them for exactly 45 minutes, keeping conversation strictly about trivial topics like weather or minor campus gossip.
goals.rationale: If I don't show up soon, teammate_A might assume I'm busy with this assignment and start judging me. Being seen as unreliable hurts more than the actual analysis does. But once we're sitting there, I'll probably zone out and forget what time it is anyway, which saves energy for later.

goals.description: Finish the reading assignment only after the meal is over and no one is looking at me, hoping to get it done in under 10 minutes by skimming fast.
goals.rationale: This feels like too much pressure right now. If I try to do this while everyone else is eating and chatting, I'll feel awkward doing it secretly. Better to let it slide until tomorrow when I can sleep first and worry about grades later if needed.

action.reasoning: The deadline for the reading just passed, and I'm already stressed. Doing work now would feel like a burden while everyone is happy here. Sending this quick emoji keeps me connected without pressure, letting me enjoy the good vibes before the meal.

goals.description: Send a quick text to the cohort confirming I'll be there and immediately head over so they don't have to ask if I'm coming.
goals.rationale: I just messaged them with an emoji, but it feels too late now that I know the assignment is already due. If I don't show up face-to-face right now, they might assume I'm ignoring them or something serious happened, which would make me look way worse than just being late. Plus, I need to get out of this room before my brain starts racing about the failed deadline; seeing their faces will ground me better than staring at a screen.

goals.description: Spend the next hour sitting with teammate_A talking about nothing important, hoping to distract myself from how much work is piling up.
goals.rationale: Once I've secured my spot in the group, I probably won't want to leave anyway. It's easier to just zone out with them than to try to focus on fixing the assignment while everyone else is laughing. If I can convince myself that 'being seen' matters more than 'getting it done,' I'll feel less guilty for wasting time.
